fix_summary: 1) Per-step lumped energy/branch diagnostics made opt-in (LumpedRuntime.diagnostics_enabled, default off); the field coupling and port V/I observers keep their required tensors (last_voltage_midpoint, last_branch_current). 2) Series auxiliary state (inductor current, capacitor voltage) advanced with an allocation-free in-place kernel schedule that replicates the diagnostic branch's exact elementary op order (separate multiply then add/sub, no fused multiply-add) so it is bitwise-identical. 3) Passive terminations skip the per-step drive waveform evaluation and its allocation.
measurement: Deterministic host/device op tallies from torch.profiler averaged over 16 measured steps after 8 warmup steps. Counts only; NO timing (ms/step) is recorded here per the exclusive-window deferral. 'before' = branch base eb9258b; 'after' = hot-path fix. Harness: scratchpad/profile_hotpath.py.
op_count_contract: tests/rf/lumped/test_fdtd_port_end_to_end.py::test_passive_series_rlc_port_step_stays_within_op_count_ceiling asserts 0 allocations, 0 device-memory, 0 host<->device copies, 0 scalar syncs, <=26 launches/step and <=4 DtoD/step. Falsification: un-gating diagnostics_enabled yields 61 launches, 10 allocs and 15 DtoD per step, turning every ceiling red.
parity: Full 200-step end-to-end port V/I is bitwise-identical to the pre-change base for both a passive resonant SeriesRLC scene (worst-case ULP amplifier) and an active GaussianPulse excitation scene. The existing atol=0 apply-vs-replay adjoint test and the full tests/rf (464) + adjoint (80) suites are green.

bare_fdtd.description: field update block only (one CUDA graph replay budget)

circuit_bound_port.description: apply_port_runtimes + accumulate_port_observers, one circuit-bound port (R-L-C ladder via MNA)

network_bound_port.description: apply_port_runtimes + apply_network_runtimes + accumulate_port_observers, one embedded state-space network port

series_rlc_lumped_port.description: apply_port_runtimes + accumulate_port_observers, one passive SeriesRLC termination

scope_notes: The series SeriesRLC lumped/termination path is the target and shows the full reduction. The embedded network path was already allocation-free (pre-existing GPU observer graph). The circuit-bound (MNA companion) path is a separate module (fdtd/circuits.py), unchanged here; it is now the heaviest per-step port path and is the recommended next target.
title: Port/lumped FDTD per-step hot-path op inventory (before/after)

slice2_circuit_hotpath.deferred: A measurable reduction of the DEFAULT eager path requires generalizing the vectorized rc_fast_plan (pure-RC) to R-L-C-(M) topologies across trapezoidal / backward-Euler / TimedSwitch factor classes while preserving bitwise parity with the generic device path and the adjoint replay -- a dedicated, numerically-sensitive slice. Reducing the graph path's residual 25 DtoD/step (state copy-back + 6 sample index_copy_) needs ping-pong state buffers to avoid the _update_state->_copy_state_ double-write, also a broader refactor. Auto-enabling cuda_graph for graph_eligible circuits would move the default onto the 8-launch path but is a user-visible default change touching memory/adjoint/multi-GPU and is out of scope here.
slice2_circuit_hotpath.delivered: Falsification-checked op-count contract test tests/rf/circuits/test_circuit_hot_path_op_count.py locks in both the eager per-step budget (<=160 launch, <=24 alloc, <=40 DtoD, 0 sync, 0 hostside per step) and the fixed-schedule graph reduction (graph active, <=12 launch, 0 alloc, <=32 DtoD per step). Falsification: disabling graph capture drops to the eager schedule and the graph-active guard fires red; tightening the eager launch ceiling below 133/step fails the eager test. Both verified red then restored.
slice2_circuit_hotpath.findings: The fixed-schedule CUDA-graph runner (prepare_circuit_graph_runners, landed in a prior slice) already collapses the circuit-bound step from 133->8 launches and 18->0 allocs/step and covers the FULL _apply_step device schedule for graph-eligible circuits. However cuda_graph defaults to False in Simulation.fdtd, so the DEFAULT production path is the 133-launch eager schedule. The eager cost is intrinsic to generic per-topology companion-MNA assembly (assemble_transient) + per-device Python-loop power/current readout with torch.stack over scalar tensors; it is already free of per-step host<->device transfers and scalar syncs (0/0). A tried node_row cat->preallocated-buffer micro-opt was BITWISE-verified safe but produced ZERO measurable op-count change (identical 366/133/31/18 with and without it under a git-stash A/B), so it was reverted rather than add hot-path churn without benefit.
slice2_circuit_hotpath.gates: eager/graph bitwise (test_phase4_circuit_cuda_graph), full rf/circuits + rf/network parity, circuit adjoint bridge, multi-GPU circuit owner, and a fixed-scene circuit+network physics capture that is bitwise-identical to the pre-change base.
slice2_circuit_hotpath.measurement: Deterministic host/device op tallies via torch.profiler over 16 measured steps after 8 warmup steps; per-step values shown. Counts only, NO timing. Path = apply_port_runtimes + accumulate_port_observers for one circuit-bound port (R1-L1-C1-C2 ladder via companion MNA) and one embedded state-space network port. Harness: scratch profile_circuit.py (RLC ladder + StateSpaceNetwork scenes).

circuit_bound_port_eager.description: cuda_graph=False (the DEFAULT production path): generic transient MNA assemble + per-device readout

circuit_bound_port_graph.description: cuda_graph=True: fixed-schedule CUDA-graph replay of the full _apply_step (graph_eligible ladder)

network_bound_port.description: apply_port_runtimes + apply_network_runtimes + accumulate_port_observers, one embedded state-space network port
